grade.description: Good DNS security with minor improvements recommended
recommendations: Address warning-level issues to improve security grade
Review and strengthen DNS security configuration

issues.recommendation: Add at least one rua= tag to your DMARC record to receive aggregate reports and monitor email authentication activity. Example: v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:dmarc@yourdomain.com;
Add CAA records to control which CAs can issue certificates for your domain

issues.description:
Missing Wildcard SPF for Subdomains

Your apex domain denies all sending with SPF -all, but no wildcard SPF record was found for *.example.com. Attackers may still spoof emails from arbitrary subdomains, undermining your brand protection.
issues.recommendation:
Add a wildcard SPF TXT record for subdomains to close the spoofing gap. For domains where subdomains should not send mail, publish:

*.example.com TXT "v=spf1 -all"

If legitimate subdomain mail exists, define SPF on those specific subdomains and avoid using a wildcard deny that would break valid sending.

details.details: Cloudflare (Edge Security, high confidence) - detected via NS: hera.ns.cloudflare.com, NS: elliott.ns.cloudflare.com
details.recommendation: No action required. If this routing is intentional, ensure the edge provider configuration matches your security and availability requirements, including origin protection and bypass prevention.

issues.description:
DNS WAF / proxy detected

Traffic for this domain is routed through: **Cloudflare**

Cloudflare (Edge Security, high confidence) - detected via NS: hera.ns.cloudflare.com, NS: elliott.ns.cloudflare.com

DNS records for this domain indicate that traffic is routed through a third-party proxy, CDN, or edge security provider, based on authoritative DNS patterns such as nameserver delegation or CNAME targets. This commonly reflects intentional use of edge services for performance, availability, or security, but DNS data alone cannot confirm whether application-layer protections are enabled or enforced.
issues.recommendation: No action is required if this routing is intentional. If unexpected, review DNS records and third-party integrations to confirm whether the proxy or edge service is still in use and remove any outdated or unnecessary configurations.

issues.description:
No TLSA Records Published

DNSSEC is enabled for this domain, but no TLSA records were found for the configured MX hosts. These MX hosts appear to be managed by a third-party email provider, so TLSA publishing may be controlled by that provider rather than by your domain's DNS zone.
issues.recommendation: No action is required unless you specifically expect DANE support from your mail provider. If DANE is required, confirm whether your provider publishes TLSA records for its SMTP infrastructure.
Configure reverse DNS (PTR records) for all IPv6 addresses to ensure proper hostname resolution and email deliverability.

issues.description:
Anomalous DNS Response Detected

Some nameservers returned unexpected responses to recursive queries. This may indicate DNS caching proxies, rate limiting, or DNS manipulation. No confirmed open recursion vulnerability was found.
issues.recommendation: Review your DNS infrastructure to ensure nameservers are properly configured. This is informational only and may not require action.
